title: The Hangover That Won't Lift — When Love Rewires Your Mind
description:
Some intoxications don't have a checkout time. This poem is about the moment love starts feeling less like a decision and more like physics — when the mind and heart stop working together and you're left without a ground to stand on or a sky to cover your head with.

Written and read by Tarun Gupta.

[TIMESTAMPS_PLACEHOLDER]

If a line from this poem reached something you've been carrying quietly, drop it in the comments. I read every one.

[LINKS_PLACEHOLDER]

Subscribe for original poems and honest reflections on the human experience every week. Each video pairs a poem with a personal story — because the best poems don't just describe feelings, they locate you inside them.
thumbnail_concept: Close-up of a single candle flame in near-darkness, soft warm amber glow. Text overlay in clean serif font: 'The Hangover That Won't Lift' — white, centered in lower third. Color mood: deep shadow, warm amber, intimate and still. No face, no person — just the flame.
end_screen_suggestion: Surface the 'When Dreams Speak of Love' poetry video or the Breath of Poetry playlist
